goal: Land the G1 result-fusion algorithm that takes a `G1Outcome` from `execute_g1` (WO-0047), merges per-source `G1ToolOutput.payload` entries by source location (file + line range), unions unique fields across sources, and resolves conflicting field values by source authority Serena > tree-sitter > ast-grep > diagnostics, per master-plan §5.1 lines 430-442.
plan_summary: Add `G1FusedLocation`, `G1FusionEntry` (per-source location-bearing payload entry shape that production wiring will adapt to in F05), `G1Conflict`, `G1FusedEntry`, `G1FusedOutcome`, and `pub fn fuse_g1(outcome: &G1Outcome) -> G1FusedOutcome` to `crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs`. The fusion algorithm: (1) for each `G1ToolOutput` with `status == G1ToolStatus::Available`, parse its `payload` as `Vec<G1FusionEntry>` (a JSON array — degraded sources contribute zero entries; a source whose `payload` cannot decode is silently skipped to keep partial-results semantics from §5.1 line 445). (2) Group entries by `G1FusedLocation { file_path, start_line, end_line }`. (3) Within each group, union the per-entry `fields` map across all contributing sources; on field-name collision with non-equal `serde_json::Value` values, the higher-authority source wins and a `G1Conflict { field, winner, losers }` row is recorded. (4) Sort fused entries by `(file_path, start_line, end_line)` so output ordering is deterministic. The frozen acceptance selector `executor::test_g1_result_fusion` lands at MODULE ROOT of `executor.rs` per DEC-0007. The test injects four local `TestG1Source` impls (per DEC-0008 §4 — `G1Source` is UCIL-owned, the WO-0047 lessons line 313 confirmed local trait impls are NOT critical-dep mocks) and exercises three sub-assertions: location-merge (3 sources hit one location, 1 source hits another → 2 fused entries), field-union (Serena and tree-sitter contribute disjoint fields at the same location → unioned superset), and authority-resolution (Serena and ast-grep contribute conflicting `signature` values → Serena wins, conflict is recorded). Production wiring of real subprocess clients into the fusion path stays deferred to P2-W7-F05 (find_references) per WO-0047 lessons line 298. Lib.rs re-exports for every new `pub` symbol land in this WO — zero deferred re-export debt (carry-forward from WO-0042 / 0043 / 0044 / 0045 / 0046 / 0047 cumulative-debt-avoidance discipline, now 6 consecutive cleared).
scope_in: Create `pub struct G1FusedLocation { pub file_path: PathBuf, pub start_line: u32, pub end_line: u32 }` with `Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Ord, PartialOrd, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize` derives. The `Hash` + `Eq` pair enables HashMap-keyed grouping; `Ord` enables deterministic output sorting. Lines are 1-based. `start_line == end_line` is permitted (single-line entries). Cite master-plan §5.1 line 430 ("merge by location") in the rustdoc.

Create `pub struct G1FusionEntry { pub location: G1FusedLocation, pub fields: serde_json::Map<String, serde_json::Value> }` with `Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize` derives. This is the per-source payload entry shape — every `G1ToolOutput.payload` for an `Available` source is expected to deserialize into `Vec<G1FusionEntry>`. Document in the rustdoc that production wiring (F05) is responsible for adapting raw source outputs (Serena hover JSON, tree-sitter AST snippets, ast-grep matches, diagnostics bundles) into this normalized shape — F02 ships only the fusion layer that consumes it.

Create `pub struct G1Conflict { pub field: String, pub winner: G1ToolKind, pub winner_value: serde_json::Value, pub losers: Vec<(G1ToolKind, serde_json::Value)> }` with `Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize` derives. Records every field where two or more sources contributed non-equal values for the same key at the same location — `winner` is the higher-authority source, `losers` is the rest in authority order. Operator-readable provenance for downstream debugging.

Create `pub struct G1FusedEntry { pub location: G1FusedLocation, pub contributing_sources: Vec<G1ToolKind>, pub fields: serde_json::Map<String, serde_json::Value>, pub conflicts: Vec<G1Conflict> }` with `Debug, Clone, Default, PartialEq, Eq, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize` derives. `contributing_sources` is sorted by authority (Serena first, diagnostics last) so a reader can spot the highest-authority contributor without scanning conflicts. `Default` derive enables the runtime-only mutation variant for AC20.

Create `pub struct G1FusedOutcome { pub entries: Vec<G1FusedEntry>, pub master_timed_out: bool, pub source_dispositions: Vec<(G1ToolKind, G1ToolStatus)> }` with `Debug, Clone, Default, PartialEq, Eq, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize` derives. `entries` is sorted by `(file_path, start_line, end_line)` for deterministic output. `master_timed_out` is forwarded from the input `G1Outcome.master_timed_out` so downstream code does not have to thread both. `source_dispositions` carries `(kind, status)` pairs in input order so a Diagnostics::Errored or Serena::TimedOut can be surfaced even though those sources contribute no entries to the fused result.

Add `pub fn fuse_g1(outcome: &G1Outcome) -> G1FusedOutcome` to `crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs` immediately AFTER `execute_g1` (around line 1158). Implementation contract:  
  (a) Build `source_dispositions: Vec<(G1ToolKind, G1ToolStatus)>` by iterating `outcome.results` in input order.  
  (b) For each `G1ToolOutput` with `status == G1ToolStatus::Available`, attempt `serde_json::from_value::<Vec<G1FusionEntry>>(output.payload.clone())`; on `Err`, log via `tracing::warn!(target = "ucil.group.structural.fusion", kind = ?output.kind, err = %e, "payload decode failed; source skipped")` and continue (partial-results semantics — a misshapen payload from one source MUST NOT poison fusion of the rest). On `Ok(entries)`, push `(output.kind, entries)` onto a working `per_source: Vec<(G1ToolKind, Vec<G1FusionEntry>)>` list.  
  (c) Group: build `BTreeMap<G1FusedLocation, Vec<(G1ToolKind, &serde_json::Map<String, Value>)>>` by location key. `BTreeMap` (NOT `HashMap`) so iteration order is the deterministic `G1FusedLocation` ordering — eliminates a sort pass at the end.  
  (d) For each `(location, contributors)` group, compute the fused entry: (i) `contributing_sources` = `contributors.iter().map(|(kind, _)| *kind).collect()` then sort by authority (Serena=0, TreeSitter=1, AstGrep=2, Diagnostics=3 — encoded by `fn authority_rank(kind: G1ToolKind) -> u8` private helper). (ii) For each unique field name across all contributors, if exactly one contributor has that field, copy its value verbatim into `fields`. If multiple contributors have the field, sort them by authority, take the highest-authority value as `winner_value`, write `winner_value` into `fields`, and (only if any of the loser values is `!= winner_value`) record a `G1Conflict { field: name, winner: highest_kind, winner_value: clone, losers: <(kind, value) pairs in authority order with values != winner_value> }`. Equal-value contributors are NOT a conflict — same answer from two sources is a corroboration, not a conflict.  
  (iii) `conflicts` is appended in field-name lexicographic order (use `BTreeMap` for the per-field grouping inside the group too, OR sort `conflicts.sort_by(|a, b| a.field.cmp(&b.field))` after collection — the former is cleaner).  
  (e) Emit `G1FusedOutcome { entries, master_timed_out: outcome.master_timed_out, source_dispositions }`.  
  Function MUST NOT panic, MUST NOT return `Result` (partial results are valid output), and MUST NOT use `.unwrap()` / `.expect()` outside `#[cfg(test)]`. Use `?` only on internal `serde_json` decode and capture the error with `match`/`if let` rather than propagating.

Add a private helper `fn authority_rank(kind: G1ToolKind) -> u8` adjacent to `fuse_g1`. Encodes `Serena=0, TreeSitter=1, AstGrep=2, Diagnostics=3` (lower number = higher authority — matches §5.1 source-authority list). Use `match` on the kind for an exhaustive compile-time check; if `G1ToolKind` later gains a variant the rank function MUST also be updated, and the `match` ensures the compiler enforces this.

Add `#[tracing::instrument(name = "ucil.group.structural.fusion", level = "debug", skip(outcome), fields(input_results = outcome.results.len(), input_master_timed_out = outcome.master_timed_out))]` on `fuse_g1` per master-plan §15.2 span naming `ucil.<layer>.<op>`. Carry-forward from WO-0047's `ucil.group.structural` parent span (the F02 fusion is the next operation under that namespace).

Add the frozen acceptance test `pub async fn test_g1_result_fusion()` annotated `#[tokio::test(flavor = "multi_thread", worker_threads = 4)]` at MODULE ROOT of `crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs` (NOT inside `mod tests {}`) per DEC-0007. The test must:  
  (a) Construct four `TestG1Source` impls (per DEC-0008 — UCIL-owned trait, local impls in test are NOT critical-dep mocks per WO-0047 lessons line 313). Each `execute` returns a `G1ToolOutput` whose `payload` deserializes to `Vec<G1FusionEntry>`. Sub-test scenario:  
    - TreeSitter source: 1 entry at `(util.rs, 10, 20)` with field `{ "ast_kind": "function" }`.  
    - Serena source: 1 entry at `(util.rs, 10, 20)` with fields `{ "signature": "fn foo() -> i32", "hover_doc": "Computes foo" }`.  
    - AstGrep source: 1 entry at `(util.rs, 10, 20)` with fields `{ "signature": "fn foo()", "pattern": "fn foo($_)" }`.  
    - Diagnostics source: 1 entry at `(util.rs, 30, 35)` with fields `{ "diagnostic": "unused variable" }`.  
  (b) Call `let raw = execute_g1(query, sources, G1_MASTER_DEADLINE).await;` followed by `let fused = fuse_g1(&raw);`.  
  (c) **Sub-assertion 1 — location merge**: `fused.entries.len() == 2` (one entry at `(util.rs, 10, 20)` aggregating TreeSitter+Serena+AstGrep, one entry at `(util.rs, 30, 35)` from Diagnostics alone). Use `assert_eq!` with a panic message that prints the actual entries.  
  (d) **Sub-assertion 2 — field union**: at the `(util.rs, 10, 20)` entry, `fields` contains `ast_kind`, `signature`, `hover_doc`, `pattern` (4 keys total). `contributing_sources` is `[Serena, TreeSitter, AstGrep]` in authority order.  
  (e) **Sub-assertion 3 — authority resolution**: at the `(util.rs, 10, 20)` entry, `fields["signature"] == "fn foo() -> i32"` (Serena's value, NOT AstGrep's `"fn foo()"`). Exactly one `G1Conflict` is recorded for the `signature` field with `winner = G1ToolKind::Serena`, `winner_value = "fn foo() -> i32"`, `losers = [(G1ToolKind::AstGrep, "fn foo()")]`. The `ast_kind` field has only one contributor (TreeSitter alone) so NO conflict is recorded for it.  
  (f) **Sub-assertion 4 — disposition pass-through**: `fused.source_dispositions.len() == 4` and every entry is `(_, G1ToolStatus::Available)`. `fused.master_timed_out == false`.  
  All assertions use `assert_eq!` with operator-readable panic messages quoting the expected vs actual values. NO `matches!()` for anything that can be expressed as `assert_eq!` (per WO-0042 lessons line 92).

Update `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs` `pub use executor::{...}` line (currently line 112) to add the 6 new public symbols: `fuse_g1`, `G1Conflict`, `G1FusedEntry`, `G1FusedLocation`, `G1FusedOutcome`, `G1FusionEntry`. Maintain the existing alphabetical-within-`executor::{}`-block ordering. Lib.rs preamble paragraph for WO-0047 (lines 63-72) gets a NEW trailing sentence appended: 'WO-0048 for `P2-W7-F02` extends [`executor`] with [`executor::fuse_g1`], the G1 fusion layer per master-plan §5.1 lines 430-442: it groups [`executor::G1Outcome`] per-source results by source location, unions unique fields, and resolves conflicting field values by source authority Serena > tree-sitter > ast-grep > diagnostics. Production wiring of real subprocess clients into the fusion path is deferred to P2-W7-F05 (`find_references`); F02 ships the fusion algorithm only.' Pre-emptively rewrites the deferral-signpost the WO-0045 lessons (line 218) called out as load-bearing — same pattern as WO-0047 Part C of scope_in.

Add `scripts/verify/P2-W7-F02.sh` per the standing convention. Script must (a) run `cargo test -p ucil-daemon executor::test_g1_result_fusion -- --nocapture`, (b) tee output through a regex matching either `cargo test`'s `test result: ok. ... 0 failed` shape OR nextest's `... tests run: ... passed` shape (alternation per WO-0042 lessons line 75 — note that nextest currently reports `... tests passed` so use a tolerant pattern: `grep -Eq 'test result: ok\. .* 0 failed|[0-9]+ tests? passed'`), (c) emit `[FAIL] P2-W7-F02: <reason>` on non-zero exit with a hint pointing at the assertion line. Make executable: `chmod +x scripts/verify/P2-W7-F02.sh`.

**Anti-cascade for `#![deny(warnings)]`**: the per-mutation patches MUST use the runtime-only variant pattern from WO-0046 lessons line 245 — `let _ = arg;` for any arg that the mutated body discards, plus a no-op return of `G1FusedOutcome::default()` (the type derives `Default`, enabling this). Document BOTH the literal sed AND the runtime-only variant in `acceptance` mutation entries below.

**Compose with `execute_g1` WITHOUT modifying it.** WO-0047's `execute_g1` is the producer; F02's `fuse_g1` is the consumer. F02 calls `execute_g1` from inside the test ONLY to construct a real `G1Outcome` — production callers of `fuse_g1` will live in F05 (`find_references` MCP tool). Do NOT change `execute_g1`'s signature, body, or any of WO-0047's pub types. The 9 WO-0047 public symbols (G1Source/G1Query/G1ToolKind/G1ToolStatus/G1ToolOutput/G1Outcome/execute_g1/G1_MASTER_DEADLINE/G1_PER_SOURCE_DEADLINE) all stay frozen. Cite this non-modification in the executor preamble doc-comment so the next reader does not double-take.

Optional but recommended rustdoc on `fuse_g1`: include a brief `# Examples` block showing a 2-source fusion case (TreeSitter + Serena at one location → fused entry with both fields, no conflict). Backticks every uppercase identifier per WO-0043 `clippy::doc_markdown` lesson — pre-flight grep `rg -nE '^\s*///.*\b[A-Z][A-Z_0-9]+\b' crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs` returns zero hits OR every match is inside backticks (per AC02).
scope_out: **No production wiring** of real Serena MCP, ast-grep MCP, real `ucil_treesitter::Parser`, or real `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics::bridge` clients. F02 ships the fusion algorithm + 4 local TestG1Source impls in the unit test ONLY. Production wiring of real subprocess clients is deferred to P2-W7-F05 (find_references) per WO-0047 lessons line 298 + line 318.

**No new MCP-tool wiring.** `find_definition` continues to use WO-0037's `enrich_find_definition` (Serena hover only). `find_references` is F05's job (depends on F02 — this WO unblocks it). `server::McpServer` is BLANKET-banned in `forbidden_paths`.

**No SCIP / Joern integration.** P2-W7-F08 lands SCIP P1; Joern is post-Phase-2. The 4-variant `G1ToolKind` (frozen by WO-0047) is the source-set for F02. `authority_rank` MUST cover all 4 variants exhaustively.

**No retry / backoff logic on the fusion path.** Fusion is a pure CPU-bound transform on the input `G1Outcome`; failure modes are bounded to `serde_json` decode errors (which we silently log and skip per partial-results semantics). No `tokio::spawn`, no `tokio::time::timeout` inside `fuse_g1` — the orchestrator (`execute_g1`) already owns the deadline guard.

**No agent-narrative synthesis.** Master-plan §5.1 lines 437-439 mention an Interpreter agent that produces `agent_narrative`; that's a Phase-3 / agent-system feature outside F02's scope. The fused output schema in §5.1 lines 440-442 names `agent_narrative`, but F02 does NOT include it — `G1FusedEntry.fields` is a free-form `serde_json::Map` so future agent-narrative synthesis can be appended without breaking the fusion layer.

**No changes to `crates/ucil-treesitter/**`, `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/**`, `crates/ucil-core/**`, `crates/ucil-cli/**`, `crates/ucil-embeddings/**`, `crates/ucil-agents/**`, `adapters/**`, `ml/**`, `plugins/**`** — F02 is single-crate (ucil-daemon), single-source-file (`executor.rs`) by design. Same shape as WO-0047.

**No new dependencies.** `serde_json` already a workspace dep, `tracing` already in use, `tokio` already in use, `std::collections::BTreeMap` is std, `serde` derives already imported. `Cargo.toml` files MUST stay unchanged — AC18 verifies this with `git diff --name-only main...HEAD -- '*.toml'` returning empty.

**No new ADR.** DEC-0007 (frozen-selector module-root placement), DEC-0008 (UCIL-internal trait boundaries OK to local-impl in tests — `G1Source` is the seam), DEC-0005 (module-coherence commits up to ~200 LOC) cover the WO. If a NEW pattern surfaces (e.g. lifetime issues with `&serde_json::Map` borrowing inside the BTreeMap, surprising serde-derive interactions on `G1Conflict`), STOP and write an ADR before working around.

**No `cargo-mutants` per-WO gate.** DEC-0007 removed it; pre-baked function-body mutations in `acceptance` are the authoritative anti-laziness layer (carry from WO-0042 / 0043 / 0044 / 0045 / 0046 / 0047, now 6 consecutive).

AC05 — Sub-assertion 1 (location merge): `fused.entries.len() == 2` when 4 sources contribute entries at 2 distinct `G1FusedLocation` keys (TreeSitter+Serena+AstGrep at `(util.rs, 10, 20)`; Diagnostics at `(util.rs, 30, 35)`). The assertion message MUST quote actual `entries` content on failure for operator-actionable diagnostics.

AC06 — Sub-assertion 2 (field union): at the `(util.rs, 10, 20)` entry, `fused.entries[0].fields` contains exactly the 4 keys `ast_kind`, `signature`, `hover_doc`, `pattern` (one from each non-conflicting source). `contributing_sources` equals `vec![G1ToolKind::Serena, G1ToolKind::TreeSitter, G1ToolKind::AstGrep]` in authority order.

AC07 — Sub-assertion 3 (authority resolution): at the `(util.rs, 10, 20)` entry, `fields["signature"]` equals Serena's value `"fn foo() -> i32"` (NOT AstGrep's `"fn foo()"`). Exactly one `G1Conflict` is recorded with `field == "signature"`, `winner == G1ToolKind::Serena`, `winner_value == "fn foo() -> i32"`, `losers == vec![(G1ToolKind::AstGrep, json!("fn foo()"))]`. The `ast_kind` field (single contributor: TreeSitter) does NOT produce a conflict entry — corroboration / single-contributor cases MUST NOT show up in `conflicts`.

AC08 — Sub-assertion 4 (disposition pass-through): `fused.source_dispositions.len() == 4` with all four entries having `G1ToolStatus::Available`; `fused.master_timed_out == false`. Order matches the input source order (TreeSitter, Serena, AstGrep, Diagnostics — same order as the test's source vec construction).

AC21 — Pre-baked mutation #1 (fusion body neutered):  
  Literal sed: `sed -i 's|pub fn fuse_g1(outcome: &G1Outcome) -> G1FusedOutcome {|pub fn fuse_g1(outcome: \&G1Outcome) -> G1FusedOutcome { let _ = outcome; return G1FusedOutcome::default();|' crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs` (prepends an early-return that discards the input).  
  Runtime-only variant (use this if literal sed produces a `#![deny(warnings)]` cascade per WO-0046 lessons line 245): replace the `fuse_g1` body's first executable statement with `let _ = outcome; return G1FusedOutcome::default();` (drops any `mut` bindings if relevant, discards the arg with `let _ = X;`, returns `Default`). The `unreachable_code` warning on the rest of the body MAY require `#[allow(unreachable_code)]` on the whole function for the mutation step — restore via `git checkout -- crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs` and the original body returns.  
  Expected runtime failure on `cargo test -p ucil-daemon executor::test_g1_result_fusion`: panic at AC05's location-merge assertion (`fused.entries.len() == 2` fails because `Default::default()` produces an empty `entries` Vec — observed length 0).  
  Restore: `git checkout -- crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs`. Rerun: green.  
  `G1FusedOutcome` MUST `derive Default` for this mutation patch to compile — bake that derive into the type definition (per scope_in line 5).

AC22 — Pre-baked mutation #2 (authority resolution disabled):  
  Literal sed: `sed -i 's|kind: G1ToolKind::Serena => 0,|kind: G1ToolKind::Serena => 3,|' crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs` (swaps Serena from highest authority to lowest — every conflict now resolves to AstGrep's value because AstGrep stays at rank 2 and Serena is now rank 3).  
  Runtime-only variant (if the `match`-arm sed pattern is fragile due to formatting): replace the `match kind` body inside `authority_rank` with `match kind { G1ToolKind::Serena => 3, G1ToolKind::TreeSitter => 1, G1ToolKind::AstGrep => 2, G1ToolKind::Diagnostics => 0 }` — Diagnostics now wins over Serena, but in this test scenario Diagnostics contributes a non-conflicting field, so the actual change is Serena (3) loses to AstGrep (2) on the `signature` field.  
  Expected runtime failure on `cargo test -p ucil-daemon executor::test_g1_result_fusion`: panic at AC07's authority-resolution assertion (`fields["signature"] == "fn foo() -> i32"` fails — actual value is `"fn foo()"` from AstGrep). The `G1Conflict` row's `winner` is `AstGrep` instead of `Serena`.  
  Restore: `git checkout -- crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs`. Rerun: green.
